evals.prompt: I want to set up gemini cli in my CI/CD pipeline on GitHub Actions so it can automatically review PRs. We use an API key stored in GitHub secrets. How do I set this up with structured JSON output so I can parse the review?
evals.expected_output: Should provide a GitHub Actions workflow snippet using GEMINI_API_KEY from secrets, -p flag for headless mode, --output-format json, and jq for parsing. Should mention --yolo if tool actions are needed.

assertions.text: Uses -p or --prompt flag for headless/non-interactive mode
Includes --output-format json flag for structured output
Shows how to set GEMINI_API_KEY from GitHub secrets
Provides a GitHub Actions workflow YAML snippet
Shows how to parse JSON response (e.g., with jq .response)
Does not invent non-existent CLI flags or options

evals.prompt: I need to connect a custom MCP server I built in Python to gemini cli. The server needs an API key from my environment. Also I want to restrict which tools it can expose. How do I configure this?
evals.expected_output: Should explain settings.json mcpServers config with command, args, env ($VAR pattern), and includeTools/excludeTools. Should mention trust field and security considerations.

assertions.text: Shows mcpServers config with command, args fields
Shows $VAR pattern for environment variable references in env field
Explains includeTools and/or excludeTools for restricting exposed tools
Mentions security considerations (trust field, not hardcoding keys, etc.)
Uses correct Python command (python/python3) in the command field

evals.prompt: How do I create a custom slash command in gemini cli that runs git diff --cached and asks the AI to write a commit message? I want it available in all my projects.
evals.expected_output: Should provide a .toml file in ~/.gemini/commands/ with !{git diff --cached} shell syntax and prompt template. Should mention headless limitation.

assertions.text: Points to ~/.gemini/commands/ for global user-scoped commands
Shows .toml file format with prompt field
Uses !{git diff --cached} shell execution syntax in the prompt
Warns that custom slash commands don't work in headless/non-interactive mode
Provides a complete, copy-pasteable .toml file example
